title: Fix or properly gate the 69 failing comms integration tests (cloud.test.ts, local.test.ts)
description:
Pre-existing: 69 of the 72 baseline UI test failures are in internal/tickboard/ui/src/comms/cloud.test.ts (32) and local.test.ts (37) — suites named "CloudCommsClient Integration" / local equivalent. Failures include basic cases like "connect() establishes WebSocket connection", suggesting the suites expect a live server/WebSocket endpoint (or a mock that has rotted). src/comms/test-setup.ts is the existing mock infrastructure — start the diagnosis there.

First DIAGNOSE: run pnpm test src/comms/cloud.test.ts, read the actual error output (connection refused? mock missing? API drift after the CommsClient refactor?). Classify: (a) tests rotted after a refactor -> fix them against current client API; (b) they genuinely need a live server -> stand up an in-process mock WebSocket/HTTP server in test-setup (vitest-compatible, e.g. mock-socket or a minimal ws server) so they run hermetically; (c) truly environment-bound -> gate behind an env var (TICKS_LIVE_TESTS=1) with describe.skipIf so the default run is green — but prefer (a)/(b); gating 69 tests is last resort and if you land on (c) explain why in your report.

Goal state: pnpm test green by default with as much real coverage retained as possible.

Files: internal/tickboard/ui/src/comms/** (tests + test-setup; client source ONLY if the diagnosis shows test-vs-API drift where the tests document intended behavior — justify any client change in your report).
Per project CLAUDE.md: pnpm only.

Run: cd internal/tickboard/ui && pnpm test
acceptance_criteria: pnpm test: 0 failures in src/comms/ by default; coverage preserved (fixed or hermetic mock) or gating justified in report; no unrelated test changes
